spec_match.rationale: Attaches an actual `clipboardchange` listener on `navigator.clipboard` and calls `clipboard.read()` inside the handler — exactly the spec shape the chromestatus entry promises.

interactive.rationale: Start/Stop/Clear controls plus a live log of every event with timestamps — past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb says hit start then copy anywhere; the page does exactly that and logs each event.

edge_cases.rationale: Includes a polyfill fallback and surfaces `read denied` errors, but never demonstrates the rate-limit / debounce behaviour that the spec discussion calls out, nor the cross-origin / no-permission path.

references.rationale: Links to the chromestatus feature URL in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from CSS variables, no raw hex, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate clipboard-read permission denial
openQuestions.detail: The watcher currently treats permission errors as a generic 'read denied' log line. Add a clearly-labelled state for when the page has not been granted the `clipboard-read` permission — the event still fires, but `.read()` rejects. Authors need to see the event-without-content path explicitly.

openQuestions.title: Show what events look like cross-tab
openQuestions.detail: A core selling point of clipboardchange is multi-tab / multi-app sync. Open two tabs and you should see both fire. Add a short paragraph or a second iframe so the user can observe propagation across same-origin tabs in one page.

openQuestions.title: Link the canonical spec URL
openQuestions.detail: The page links only to chromestatus. The feature index has a spec PR link (`github.com/w3c/clipboard-apis/pull/239`); the concept page should too, so visitors who land here from search can dig into the actual algorithm.

summary: Honest minimal watcher that proves the event fires. Misses two interesting edge cases — explicit permission-denied paths and cross-tab propagation — and doesn't link the spec PR. Strong baseline, light depth.
